chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Spl. Cr. Appeal No. D – 25 of 2024 (Jinsar Ali Kanhar versus The State) Present: Mr. Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J. Mr. Arbab Ali Hakro, J. Date of hearing: 28.03.2024 Mr. Saeed Jamal Lund, Advocate for appellant. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, Deputy Prosecutor General. O R D E R Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J. – Through this order, MA No.145/2024 filed by appellant / applicant U/S 426 CrPC is decided. 2. Applicant has been convicted and sentenced to suffer rigorous imprisonment for three years and to pay fine of Rs.10,000/- for keeping in his possession 1300 grams of hemp / bhang, u/s 9(b) of CNS Act. 3. Learned Counsel submits that his sentence is short. Appellant is a young boy of 21/22 years old, and his incarceration in jail would jeopardize his chances of success in the future. Learned Deputy Prosecutor General has extended no objection on the ground of short sentence. 4. Accordingly, this application is allowed, and the sentence of appellant is suspended. He is granted bail in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. 5. So far the appeal is concerned, same is adjourned to a date in office. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
